description: Linear regression and time-series forecast functions that fit a least-squares line to a rolling price window, extracting slope, intercept, endpoint, and one-bar-ahead forecast values

fields.name: real_prices
fields.label: Price series (typically Close; chronological double array)

fields.name: time_period
fields.label: Time Period — rolling window length (default 14; range 2-100000)

fields.name: out_values
fields.label: Output array (double values; units depend on indicator_type)

linearreg.formula: Least-squares line over the window [i-n+1 .. i]; output is the VALUE of that line at bar i
linearreg.use_case: Dynamic trend line value; substitute for moving average with better fit to recent data

interpretation.above_price: Price above LINEARREG line indicates upward trend bias
interpretation.slope_direction: Direction of line shift indicates momentum

linearreg_angle.formula: angle = atan(slope) * (180 / pi) — slope of the regression line converted to degrees

interpretation.flat: Angle near 0° — consolidation or sideways trend

linearreg_intercept.formula: y-intercept of least-squares line for window ending at bar i (value at the start of the window)
linearreg_intercept.use_case: Rarely used directly; building block for computing slope and regression line

linearreg_slope.formula: slope = (n * sum(x*y) - sum(x) * sum(y)) / (n * sum(x^2) - sum(x)^2)  where x = bar index, y = price

interpretation.positive: Positive slope — price trending up over the window

tsf.formula: TSF[i] = LINEARREG[i] + LINEARREG_SLOPE[i]  — adds one more step to the endpoint
tsf.use_case: One-bar-ahead price forecast based on linear trend; used as a dynamic support/resistance level
tsf.note: TSF leads LINEARREG by exactly one bar in trend direction; not a true predictive model
tsf.warning: TSF assumes price continues on the current linear slope — fails at inflection points

compute_success.result: Output array aligned to input via outBegIdx; caller must offset by timePeriod-1 bars

insufficient_data.result: No output; provide at least timePeriod bars

invalid_period.result: Period must be at least 2 for a meaningful regression line

errors.message: Not enough bars. Provide at least timePeriod data points.

anti_patterns.rule: Do not treat TSF as a genuine price forecast model
anti_patterns.why: TSF extrapolates one bar along the current linear slope — it is a trend projection, not a predictive model; it will fail at every turning point by definition

anti_patterns.rule: Do not use LINEARREG_ANGLE with very short periods
anti_patterns.why: Short windows make the angle highly sensitive to individual bars — a single outlier candle can swing the angle by 20-30 degrees; use period >= 10 for stability

anti_patterns.rule: Do not compare LINEARREG values across different period lengths
anti_patterns.why: Longer periods smooth out noise and produce a less reactive value; same price series, different periods produce different regression endpoints — not directly comparable

anti_patterns.rule: Do not use these functions as the sole signal source in automated strategies
anti_patterns.why: Linear regression assumes a linear price model; real markets have non-linear regimes; combine with ADX or volatility filters to detect when the assumption breaks down

extensions.output_type: Double Array — same price unit as input (except LINEARREG_ANGLE which outputs degrees)
